name: Habib Mousa
description:
'''Habib Mousa''' ({{Langx|syr|ܚܒܝܒ ܡܘܣܐ}}; born 9 October 1952) is a Syrian musician. From an early childhood, he was interested in music until his formal career began in 1968 with the release of "Shamo Mar". 

Mousa remains one of the most popular [[Western Assyrians|Western Assyrian]] musicians to date,{{Sfn|Lundberg|1998|p=22}} and is referred to as the "King of the Suryoyto Song" for his influence in secular [[Assyrian folk-pop music]].{{Sfn|Atto|2011|p=297}} He is considered the second person to compose and sing songs in [[Classical Syriac]] (Kthbonoyo/Lishana Atiqa). His music is additionally played on [[Swedish Radio]] and has appeared on television and at parties. Alongside other musicians as [[Joseph Malke]], he has worked to bring Assyrians together in organizations through the use of music.{{Sfn|Lundberg|1998|p=23}}
